records.ety: [F. dvorer, fr. L. devorare; de + vorare to eat greedily, swallow up. See Voracious.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Devoured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Devouring.]

defs: To eat up with greediness; to consume ravenously; to feast upon like a wild beast or a glutton; to prey upon.
quotes: Some evil beast hath devoured him.

defs: To seize upon and destroy or appropriate greedily, selfishly, or wantonly; to consume; to swallow up; to use up; to waste; to annihilate.
quotes: Famine and pestilence shall devour him.
I waste my life and do my days devour.

defs: To enjoy with avidity; to appropriate or take in eagerly by the senses.
quotes: Longing they look, and gaping at the sight, Devour her o'er with vast delight.
